title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, and Production Defense · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs openapi 3.2, compatibility, deployment, and production defense from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the versioned API contract and production release defense. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned API contract and production release defense after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Describe OpenAPI 3.2 operations, servers, parameters, request bodies, responses, problems, schemas, security, callbacks, and webhooks with tool limits.
Compare specification, route registration, transport types, validators, examples, tests, auth policy, and observed responses to detect bidirectional drift.
Classify additive and breaking changes across fields, nullability, validation, status, errors, ordering, auth, caching, and behavior; then plan deprecation and migration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Openapi32 Contract · changed case · Review Openapi32 Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review team handles case 2667 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate one OpenAPI-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and rollback release; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Describe OpenAPI 3.2 operations, servers, parameters, request bodies, responses, problems, schemas, security, callbacks, and webhooks with tool limits.

Which action demonstrates Openapi32 Contract without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Go server Openapi32 Contract · changed case · Review Openapi32 Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for describe openapi 3.2 operations, servers, parameters, request bodies, responses, problems, schemas, security, callbacks, and webhooks with tool limits. For Go server Openapi32 Contract · changed case · Review Openapi32 Contract, keep Openapi32 Contract connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.title: Go server Deploy Migrate Drain Rollback · changed case · Review Deploy Migrate Drain Rollback
